subject: New York
body:
Dear JE,
I need to give MAC Capital 12-18 months to have something solid on my CV,
but then I'm out of here. As of now, my plan is to return to New York or go
to London.



*Dubai diary:*
 +30 C so still a bit on the chilly side...

*New York, New York*
I don't want to hear "what did I tell you".

I woke up in anxiety: I miss New York! I want to move back to New York! This
town is suffocating me. I can't stay in this desert-town with its
50-levels, futuristic towers; oversized SUVs; and Arabs who have more money
than God, and me, fighting myself bloody with one of them.

I need to leave this desert-Mickey-Mouse-wanna-be-town.

*Miraj - an illusion*
I went to Miraj, an Islamic museum where all objects are for sale.
Supposedly, it's shockingly expensive, but for someone who spent seven years
in New York and saw exhibitions where a drawer costs $100,000; $10,000 for a
huge, antique table doesn't seem that much...

*Miscellaneous*
I managed to get through this month without any speeding tickets!

*Dubai is a fishbowl*
Dubai is so small that after a year I've started recycling men. Back to the
very first guy I dated here. I'm also back to remembering why I shouldn't
have dated him to begin with.

It feels a bit retro to date a Lebanese. I've put a few nationalities
between myself and the Lebanese by now: Saudis and Croations; recycled
Saudis...

Since Dubai is so small, the guys I've dated are around. They're not
disappearing like in New York where the city just absorbs them. In Dubai it
doesn't matter if you lose track of them, because you'll bump into them
professionally or socially.

(In Dubai there are only 100,000 people in my social circle. The remaining
1.3 million are ... not in my social circle...)

*Work*
People keep asking me about work. It's investment banking, ok. It's boring,
ok. It will not help mankind. End of transmission.

Take care and God bless,
-- 
Cecilia Steen
Dubai Mobile +971 50 940 2062
body_html:
Dear JE,
I need to give MAC Capital 12-18 months to have something solid on my CV, but then I'm out of here. As of now, my plan is to return to New York or go to London. 
 
 
 
Dubai diary: 
+30 C so still a bit on the chilly side...
 
New York, New York 
I don't want to hear "what did I tell you".
 
I woke up in anxiety: I miss New York! I want to move back to New York! This town is suffocating me. I can't stay in this desert-town with its 50-levels, futuristic towers; oversized SUVs; and Arabs who have more money than God, and me, fighting myself bloody with one of them.
 
I need to leave this desert-Mickey-Mouse-wanna-be-town.
 
Miraj - an illusion
I went to Miraj, an Islamic museum where all objects are for sale. Supposedly, it's shockingly expensive, but for someone who spent seven years in New York and saw exhibitions where a drawer costs $100,000; $10,000 for a huge, antique table doesn't seem that much...
 
Miscellaneous
I managed to get through this month without any speeding tickets!
 
Dubai is a fishbowl
Dubai is so small that after a year I've started recycling men. Back to the very first guy I dated here. I'm also back to remembering why I shouldn't have dated him to begin with.
 
It feels a bit retro to date a Lebanese. I've put a few nationalities between myself and the Lebanese by now: Saudis and Croations; recycled Saudis...
 
Since Dubai is so small, the guys I've dated are around. They're not disappearing like in New York where the city just absorbs them. In Dubai it doesn't matter if you lose track of them, because you'll bump into them professionally or socially.
 
(In Dubai there are only 100,000 people in my social circle. The remaining 1.3 million are ... not in my social circle...)
 
Work
People keep asking me about work. It's investment banking, ok. It's boring, ok. It will not help mankind. End of transmission.
  
Take care and God bless,
--
Cecilia Steen

subject: New York
body:
Jeffrey,

Hope you are very well. I am sooooo excited to be coming to New York and
thanks so much for making this happen!

My dates are this Thursday February 21st - Saturday March 1st.

Please let me know when it will be a good time for us to meet up. Can't
wait.

Yippee!

See you soon,

Emmy x
